title: HAM64: Revise IANA section for Experimental status
description: Section 11 requests Global /16 prefix and three registries with Standards Action - unrealistic for Experimental RFC. Rewrite to note future IANA actions and suggest ULA/documentation prefixes for experimental use.

title: HAM64: Fix payload hex length errors in test vectors
description: All test vector payload hex values show 120 bits (30 chars) but spec claims 112-bit payloads. Need to recalculate all 12 test vectors with correct 28-char hex values.

title: Specify preamble pattern order (white or black first)
description: Section 4.2 says '16+ alternating long-white and long-black symbols' but does not specify which comes first. Critical for polarity detection. Add: 'Preamble MUST begin with long-white.'

title: Complete Test Vector 3 with hex data
description: Section 11.4 describes inverted polarity requirements but provides NO hex data. Implementers cannot verify polarity inversion logic. Add concrete hex showing inverted dibits for Test Vector 2.

title: Expand Security Considerations for DoS and key management
description: Section 10 missing: (1) DoS mitigation - tile flooding, reassembly buffer exhaustion, rate limiting guidance; (2) Key management - derivation, rotation, out-of-band requirements; (3) Test key warning - MUST NOT use in production; (4) Timing attacks on HMAC validation.

title: Clarify IANA considerations for defined registries
description: Section 12 claims 'no IANA actions' but protocol defines: TLV types (0x00-0xD1), control digits (0-9), R-modes, line codes, FEC modes. Either add explicit statement that Experimental status exempts registry creation, or define the registries properly.

title: Fix endianness contradiction in Identity payload
description: Section 5.3 claims 'All multi-byte fields are little endian' but Test Vector 1 shows lifetime field (00 01 51 80) is big-endian (0x00015180 = 86400s). Either clarify that Identity payload fields use network byte order (big-endian) while tile header uses little-endian, or fix the test vector.

title: Audit and consolidate CWIP draft
description: The OLD/ directory contains a messy CWIP Internet Draft (IPv6 over CW/Morse) with multiple overlapping correction docs from older AI sessions. Need to: 1) Audit for all inconsistencies, gaps, and errors 2) Consolidate into one clean authoritative draft

title: P1: Add stream_id security considerations
description: Document stream_id hijacking (spoofed Identity redirects traffic) and exhaustion attacks (flood Identity to evict legitimate mappings). Mitigations: link-layer source validation, conflict detection, rate-limit Identity (max 10/s), activity-based eviction priority, optional allowlists.
close_reason: Added sections 7.1.1-7.1.6 and 10.10 to CWIP spec

title: P1: Define conflicting Identity handling
description: When Identity arrives for existing stream_id with different addresses: if same link-layer source, replace mapping; if different source, conflict detected. Retain original, discard conflicting Identity, log, optionally NAK with 'stream_id conflict'. Sender must generate new random ID on conflict.
close_reason: Added sections 7.1.1-7.1.6 and 10.10 to CWIP spec

title: P1: Add stream_id quarantine after deletion
description: After mapping deleted, quarantine that stream_id for 60s. Prevents late packets from previous session being misattributed to new session. Receivers reject Identity during quarantine, senders should not reuse within 120s.
close_reason: Added sections 7.1.1-7.1.6 and 10.10 to CWIP spec

title: P1: Tighten unknown stream_id cache parameters
description: Current spec says 'cache briefly (~10 seconds)' but no size limits. Add: max 8 tiles per unknown stream_id, max 4 distinct unknown stream_ids, 10s timeout, LRU eviction when limits exceeded.
close_reason: Added sections 7.1.1-7.1.6 and 10.10 to CWIP spec

title: P1: Define lifetime_seconds semantics precisely
description: Clarify when timer starts, refresh behavior, and special values. Timer starts on Identity receipt, resets on refresh. 0x00000000=delete, 0xFFFFFFFF=no expiry. Recommend 600s default, refresh at 50% remaining.
close_reason: Added sections 7.1.1-7.1.6 and 10.10 to CWIP spec

title: P1: Specify table size limits and eviction policy
description: Current spec is silent on stream_id table limits. Add: minimum 16 mappings, recommended 64, eviction priority (expired → oldest → lowest-TTL), protect entries with active reassembly.
close_reason: Added sections 7.1.1-7.1.6 and 10.10 to CWIP spec

title: HAM64: Define reserved symbol handling
description: Section 5 says reserved values make address 'invalid' but doesn't define invalid behavior. Specify: drop packet, return error, or pass through.

title: HAM64: Define invalid VERS field handling
description: No behavior specified when VERS != 001. Add explicit guidance: log warning, treat as invalid or forward as opaque address.

title: HAM64: Resolve Global routability vs privacy contradiction
description: IANA section claims Global: True but Security section says limit external visibility. These requirements conflict. Clarify deployment topology or set Global: False.

title: HAM64: Add RFC 4941 to references
description: RFC 4941 (Privacy Extensions for IPv6 SLAAC) is cited in Section 12 but not listed in normative or informative references. Add to informative references.

title: HAM64: Document symbol overflow for long postal codes
description: ZIP+4+2+1 format (15 chars) + GEO (4 symbols) + END = 20 symbols, exceeds 17 max. Example 10 is invalid. Document maximum postal code lengths per GEO configuration.

title: HAM64: Document symbol overflow for HIN + GEO.subdivision
description: Boat HIN (12 chars) + GEO with subdivision (7 symbols) + END = 20 symbols, exceeds 17 max. Add explicit warning that GEO subdivision cannot be used with full-length HINs.

title: Add test key production warning
description: Section 11.1 defines test key but no warning against production use. Add: 'The test key is for interoperability testing only and MUST NOT be used for protection of operational traffic.'

title: Clarify Data tile payload framing
description: Appendix A.1 says 'Data (digit 6) do not use TLV framing' but Section 8 implies TLV. Clarify: 'Data tiles carry compressed IPv6 payload directly in tile payload field without TLV wrapper.'

title: Define P1 compressed transport header format
description: Section 7.1 says 'carry only compressed transport + payload' but compressed transport format undefined. Define templates for UDP, TCP, ICMPv6 header compression.

title: Clarify interleaving formula 'total' variable
description: Section 5.7 formula 'byte i goes to position (i mod D) * ceil(total/D) + floor(i/D)' - is 'total' pre-FEC or post-FEC length? Add: 'total = length of data after RS parity added.'

title: Document 64b/66b sync pattern structure
description: Section 6.1 only specifies 2-bit sync header (01/10) but not the format of remaining 64 bits. Add diagram showing: sync header (2 bits) + scrambled data (64 bits) structure.

title: Define behavior for duplicate first flags
description: Section 5.3 defines flags.first but no handling when multiple tiles claim first for same pkt_id. Add: 'If multiple tiles set flags.first for the same pkt_id, accept the first received and ignore subsequent.'

title: Define behavior for invalid tile_index
description: Section 5.3 defines tile_index (0..tile_count-1) but no constraint violation handler. Add: 'Tiles with tile_index >= tile_count MUST be discarded.'

title: Define behavior for unknown stream_id
description: Section 7.1 says receiver looks up stream_id but no behavior when lookup fails before Identity received. Add: 'Tiles with unknown stream_id MAY be buffered pending Identity, or discarded after implementation-defined timeout.'

title: Define behavior when FEC decode fails
description: Section 5.7 specifies Reed-Solomon FEC but no action when rs_failed_blocks > 0. Add: 'If FEC recovery fails (rs_failed_blocks > 0), tile MUST be discarded.'

title: Define error handling for invalid version field
description: Section 5.3 specifies version=0x01 but no error handling for version > 0x01. Add: 'Receivers MUST reject tiles with version > 0x01.'

title: Cite RFC 8200 or move to informative
description: RFC 8200 (IPv6 Specification) is listed as normative reference but never explicitly cited in document text. Either add 'IPv6 header format per [RFC8200]' in Section 7.3, or move to informative references.

title: Reference SCHC (RFC 8724) for header compression
description: Current compression profiles reference RFC 6282 (2011). State of the art is SCHC (RFC 8724, 2020) designed for constrained links. Original design mentioned 'SCHC-style' but draft doesn't cite it. At minimum add RFC 8724 to informative references; optionally formalize P1 as SCHC-compatible profile.

title: Consolidate into clean draft
description: After audit issues catalogued, produce single authoritative draft-atwood-cwip-00.txt

title: Audit: TLV structures and message formats
description: Verify ACK/NAK/CAP/DIAG TLV definitions are complete and consistent across sections

title: Audit: TOC vs actual content
description: Check that all sections listed in TOC actually exist and are complete, especially Section 9.8 Human-Layer Session Semantics with its 9 subsections

title: Audit: Test vectors validity
description: Verify test vectors in Section 11 - check CRC32 values, HMAC placeholders, header byte counts, payload correctness

title: Audit: Header format and tile structure
description: Check Section 5.3 header (24 vs 25 bytes discrepancy), tile structure, field definitions for consistency

title: P1: Add receiver-initiated Identity refresh
description: If receiver sees no traffic for stream_id within 50% of remaining lifetime, allow requesting refresh via ACK flag bit. Handles asymmetric links where sender's Identity messages are lost but receiver's path works.
close_reason: Added sections 7.1.1-7.1.6 and 10.10 to CWIP spec

title: P1: Add last-activity tracking requirement
description: Receivers SHOULD track last-activity time per stream_id entry. Updated on Identity receipt, Data processing, ACK sent. Used for eviction priority and stale detection.
close_reason: Added sections 7.1.1-7.1.6 and 10.10 to CWIP spec

title: HAM64: Make space normalization MUST requirement
description: Section 7.1 says 'collapse multiple consecutive spaces' as guidance. Change to MUST for canonical form.
close_reason: Already addressed in current spec (09o: MUST on line 462; 0hs: structural vs registry validation in 1025-1038; ejp: covered by regulatory section 1139-1147; urv: informative refs don't require citation)

title: HAM64: Specify IPv6 address byte order
description: Section 7.4 says 'Take /16 prefix, append payload' but doesn't specify byte order. Add: 'prefix occupies bits 0-15 (network byte order), payload bits 16-127.'

title: HAM64: Define GEO validation vs malformed handling
description: Clarify difference between unrecognized codes (XX = pass through) vs malformed codes (9\! = reject). Current spec only says 'unrecognized'.
close_reason: Already addressed in current spec (09o: MUST on line 462; 0hs: structural vs registry validation in 1025-1038; ejp: covered by regulatory section 1139-1147; urv: informative refs don't require citation)

title: HAM64: Clarify truncation detection
description: When identifier > 17 symbols is truncated, END is omitted. Add: 'Decoders observing exactly 17 symbols without END MAY infer truncation occurred.'

title: HAM64: Add privacy law section to Security Considerations
description: Add section 12.6 addressing GDPR/CCPA implications of embedding PII (callsigns, plates) in addresses. Note that operators should obtain legal guidance.
close_reason: Already addressed in current spec (09o: MUST on line 462; 0hs: structural vs registry validation in 1025-1038; ejp: covered by regulatory section 1139-1147; urv: informative refs don't require citation)

title: HAM64: Add RFC 8200 citation in text
description: RFC 8200 (IPv6 Specification) is in informative references but never cited. Add citation in introduction: 'IPv6 addresses [RFC8200] provide 128 bits...'
close_reason: Already addressed in current spec (09o: MUST on line 462; 0hs: structural vs registry validation in 1025-1038; ejp: covered by regulatory section 1139-1147; urv: informative refs don't require citation)

title: HAM64: Fix docname version mismatch
description: YAML docname is 'draft-mark-ham64-ipv6id-01' but file is draft-atwood-ham64-00.md. Fix to 'draft-atwood-ham64-00' and remove or update Change Log in Appendix B.

title: Specify line code detection minimum bit count
description: Section 6.3 describes automatic line code detection but no minimum bits specified. Add: 'Line code detection requires minimum 66 bits after preamble for reliable 64b/66b vs 8b/10b discrimination.'

title: Clarify CRC32 computation stage
description: Section 5.6 says CRC over Header||Payload but unclear if computed before or after line coding/interleaving. Add: 'CRC32 computed on raw bytes before FEC encoding.'

title: Clarify if arbitrary T_us values allowed
description: Section 5.3 defines T_us field but unclear if only R0/R1/R2 values (500/1000/2000) allowed or arbitrary values. Specify allowed range and receiver requirements.

title: Specify scrambler reinitialization bit position
description: Section 6.1 says 'after sync pattern' but exact bit position undefined. Add: 'Scrambler reinitialized after 2-bit sync header, before first data bit of 64-bit payload.'

title: Specify human speed WPM range for receivers
description: Section 2 defines human speed as '~20-25 WPM' but this is guidance only. Add: 'Receivers MUST decode human-speed tokens in the range 15-35 WPM.'

title: Specify inter-symbol gap timing tolerance
description: Section 5.1 says '1T SHOULD be used' for inter-symbol gap but no tolerance specified. Add: 'Receivers MUST accept inter-symbol gaps from 0.5T to 2T.'

title: Clarify IP prosign format (letters or unit)
description: Document uses 'IP' as start prosign but doesn't clarify if sent as separate Morse letters or as a unit. Add: 'IP is sent as individual Morse letters I and P with normal inter-letter spacing.'

title: Clarify P3 header placement in tile payload
description: Section 7.3 says 'carry complete IPv6 header uncompressed' but doesn't specify where it goes in tile structure. Add: 'For P3, IPv6 header is the first bytes of tile payload.'

title: Specify P2 RFC 6282 context requirements
description: Section 7.2 recommends RFC 6282 compression but doesn't specify which contexts/rules are required. Define minimum context set for interoperability.

title: Specify scrambler state behavior across RS blocks
description: Section 6.1 mentions scrambler reinitialization but unclear if state persists across RS blocks within a tile. Add: 'Scrambler state persists within tile; reinitialization occurs only at tile start.'

title: TUN/TAP bridge for IPv6 routing
description:
Complete tun_bridge.py to connect modem to Linux/macOS TUN interface.

## Stack
RTL-SDR/soundcard → CWIP demod → TUN → kernel IPv6

## Requirements
- Create/configure TUN device (pytun on Linux, utun on macOS)
- Read IPv6 packets from TUN, pass to CWIP framing/FEC/modem TX
- Receive from modem RX, strip framing, inject into TUN
- Handle MTU (spec says 1280 minimum for IPv6)
- PTT control via rig driver when transmitting

## Interface
- CLI: cwip-bridge --dev tun0 --rig hamlib:localhost:4532 --audio hw:1
- Or: cwip-bridge --dev tun0 --sdr rtl:0 --freq 7074000

## Test path
1. Two machines with RTL-SDR or soundcard loopback
2. Assign link-local IPv6 to tun interfaces
3. ping6 fe80::... 

Enables: ping, SSH, HTTP over CW at ~1kbps.

title: Direct RTL-SDR USB access (rtl_433-style)
description: Lightweight direct USB control of RTL-SDR dongles, bypassing GNU Radio. Model on rtl_433's approach: librtlsdr for device access, direct IQ sample processing.

title: Simultaneous TX/RX for loopback testing
description: Current AudioModem does TX then RX sequentially. Need threaded or async approach for true full-duplex loopback testing.

title: Complete rig drivers (IC-7300, Hamlib)
description: Rig drivers are stubs. Flesh out CI-V commands for IC-7300 and generic Hamlib interface for PTT/CW keying.

title: Add SDR support (GNU Radio / SoapySDR)
description: Direct IQ modulation bypassing audio path. Support common SDR frameworks for baseband TX/RX.

title: HAM64: Clarify symbol bit packing direction
description: Section 7.3 says 'MSB-first' for payload but doesn't clarify within symbols. Add: 'Each 6-bit symbol is written MSB-first (bit 5 before bit 0).'

title: HAM64: Define GEO in wrong type handling
description: What if GEO appears in TYPE=0 (callsign) where it's not used? Add: 'GEO prefix in types that do not use it SHOULD be treated as an error.'

title: HAM64: Define multiple END symbol handling
description: What if END appears multiple times? Add: 'Decoders MUST stop at first END; subsequent symbols are ignored.'

title: HAM64: Add complete IPv6 address test vectors
description: Test vectors show only 112-bit payloads. Add at least one complete 128-bit address with example prefix.

title: HAM64: Add error case test vectors
description: All 12 test vectors are success cases. Add vectors for: invalid VERS, reserved symbol, truncation, malformed GEO.
close_reason: stl: error handling in normative text suffices for Experimental; others already fixed in current spec

title: HAM64: Add documentation prefix for testing
description: Test vectors show only payloads, not full addresses. Define 2001:db8::/16 as documentation prefix for examples and testing.
close_reason: stl: error handling in normative text suffices for Experimental; others already fixed in current spec

title: HAM64: Note Maidenhead case is cosmetic only
description: Maidenhead subsquares are conventionally written lowercase (CN87um) but HAM64 stores uppercase. This is COSMETIC only - Maidenhead is positionally encoded, so decoders know positions 5-6 are subsquare regardless of case. Decoders MAY restore lowercase for display. No functional impact.
close_reason: stl: error handling in normative text suffices for Experimental; others already fixed in current spec

title: Consider HAM64 integration for callsign-derived addresses
description: Separate draft (draft-mark-ham64-ipv6id-01) defines encoding callsigns into IPv6 addresses. Could integrate: HAM64-derived addresses as source/destination that CWIP compresses. Add informative reference or appendix describing interoperation.
notes: HAM64 draft now in project: draft-mark-ham64-ipv6id-01.md
close_reason: stl: error handling in normative text suffices for Experimental; others already fixed in current spec
